project_summary: "EcoRoute" is a smart, community-driven navigation app designed to help urban commuters reduce their carbon footprint while saving time and money. Unlike standard GPS apps, EcoRoute analyzes real-time traffic data, public transit schedules, and weather conditions to offer multi-modal travel options—like combining a short e-scooter ride with a subway trip—prioritizing the lowest-emission paths. Users earn "Eco-Points" for choosing green routes, which they can redeem for discounts at local sustainable businesses, while a built-in social feed allows friends to compete in weekly carbon-saving challenges.

issues.title: Initialize Monorepo Structure
issues.description: Set up the root directory structure for the EcoRoute monorepo, separating the frontend (React) and backend (Express) applications to ensure clean dependency management.

setup_tasks: Initialize package.json in root for workspace management
testing_tasks: Ensure sub-folders are recognized as separate modules
documentation_tasks: Update README with project structure overview

criteria: Root directory contains client and server folders
Git ignores node_modules and build artifacts

issues.notes: Use npm workspaces or yarn workspaces to manage dependencies from the root.

issues.title: Define PostgreSQL Database Schema
issues.description: Design and implement the initial database schema using an ORM (Prisma) to support Users, Routes, Points, and Challenges.
issues.scope: Database schema definition and migration files.

criteria: All four tables exist in the database

issues.notes: Ensure foreign key constraints are properly set for user-related data.

issues.title: Implement Environment Configuration

issues.title: Develop Multi-modal Dijkstra Implementation
issues.description: Implement a graph-based routing algorithm that considers transit, walking, and cycling nodes with weighted costs.

Test edge cases with missing transit links
documentation_tasks: Document algorithm complexity and cost parameters

criteria: Algorithm returns shortest path based on carbon cost
Supports switching between transit and walking modes

issues.notes: Focus on performance; consider caching frequent routes.

issues.title: Create Carbon Savings Calculation Service
issues.description: Build a service that calculates the difference in CO2 emissions between a standard car route and the selected multi-modal route.

setup_tasks: Define emission factor constants per transport mode

criteria: Points awarded correlate directly to CO2 saved
Calculation handles floating point precision correctly

recommended_commits: fix: handle precision in point math
issues.notes: Use a standard library for decimal math to avoid floating point errors.
